Article.ArticleTitle: Educational practices for promoting healthy hydration in primary education in the face of climate change: a systematic review of the literature.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Department of Education Sciences, Universitat de Lleida, C/ Av. Estudi General 4, Lleida, 25001, Spain. laura.fornons@udl.cat.

Grant.Agency: Department of Research and Universities, the Department of Climate Action, Food and Rural Agenda, and the Climate Fund of the Generalitat de Catalunya
Department of Research and Universities, the Department of Climate Action, Food and Rural Agenda, and the Climate Fund of the Generalitat de Catalunya
Department of Research and Universities, the Department of Climate Action, Food and Rural Agenda, and the Climate Fund of the Generalitat de Catalunya
Department of Research and Universities, the Department of Climate Action, Food and Rural Agenda, and the Climate Fund of the Generalitat de Catalunya

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Declarations. Ethics approval and consent to participate: The project HIDROPONENT received a favourable approval from the Comité de Ética de Investigación y Transferencia, University of Lleida, on 9 December 2025, reference number CERT198. The present manuscript is a systematic review of published literature and did not involve human participants or the collection of new personal data; therefore, consent to participate was not applicable. Consent for publication: Not applicable. Competing interests: The authors declare no competing interests.

Reference.Citation: Kalhoff H, Sinningen K, Belgardt A, Kersting M, Luecke T. Climate change and fluid status in children: early education as one response to an emerging public health problem. Public Health Nutr. 2023;26(12):2891–4. https://doi.org/10.1017/S1368980023002562.
